items.text: The first book in the Sprawl Trilogy won what three awards?
items.reference: Nebula Award, the Philip K. Dick Award, and the Hugo Award

items.text: What movies has Juno Temple starred in?
items.reference: She has appeared in films such as " Black Mass", "The Other Boleyn Girl", "Wild Child", "Atonement", "Maleficent", "The Three Musketeers" and "The Dark Knight Rises".

items.text: Which mountain is higher, Tongshanjiabu or Himalchuli?
items.reference: Himalchuli has three main peaks: East (7893 m), West (7540 m) and North (7371 m)

items.text: Who had more of their English novels turned into Oscar-nominated films, Raja Rao or Pat Conroy?
items.reference: Two of his novels, "The Prince of Tides" and "The Great Santini", were made into Oscar-nominated films.

items.text: The brother of Ferdinand Alfred Friedrich Jodl served as what position in World War II?
items.reference: Chief of the Operations Staff of the Armed Forces High Command

items.text: Which areas of the United States were still able to deny sames sex marriages after the case in which Edith "Edie" Windsor was the main plaintiff?
items.reference: American Samoa, but not on all Native American tribal lands

items.text: Was the famous opera comique that was based on a Prosper Merimee novella a comedy or a tragedy?
items.reference: "Carmen", perhaps the most famous "opéra comique", is a tragedy

items.text: The Church of St Peter in Pertenhall, Bedfordshire, England was added to this register on 13 July 1964.
items.reference: Statutory List of Buildings of Special Architectural or Historic Interest

items.text: In what country did Petrus van Hooijdonk compete in for the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
items.reference: It was held in France from 10 June to 12 July 1998.

items.text: Which film came out first, Still Kicking: Six Artistic Women of Project Arts & Longevity or Time Bombs?
items.reference: Still Kicking: Six Artistic Women of Project Arts & Longevity

items.text: What conference champions were putted against each other in the first NBA Finals in which the Orlando Magic's played?
items.reference: Eastern Conference champion Orlando Magic against the Western Conference champion Houston Rockets.

items.reference: the most influential private citizen in the America of his day"

items.text: Which opera has more acts, La jolie fille de Perth or Mitridate, re di Ponto?
items.reference: La jolie fille de Perth "(The Fair Maid of Perth)" is an opera in four acts

items.text: What job did Jörgen Smit work, where he was working in the world center for the anthroposophical movement?
items.reference: member of the Executive Council of the General Anthroposophical Society

items.text: Kresy, which roughly was a part of the land beyond the so-called Curson Line, was drawn for what reason?
items.reference: demarcation line between the newly emerging states, the Second Polish Republic, and the Soviet Union

items.text: What date was the movie originally supposed to be released that was delayed due to the unlawful distribution of private photographs of Edison Chen with various women?
items.reference: The film was originally set to be released in May 2008

items.text: What was Thomas Boutflower Bennet's role at The Collegiate School of St Peter?
items.reference: schoolmaster at J. L. Young's Adelaide Educational Institution and at Saint Peter's College.

items.text: Q1: Which mountain is located in China, Kirat Chuli or Kangpenqing?
items.reference: Kangpenqing (also Gang Benchhen) is a mountain in the Baiku Himalayas of Tibet, China.

items.text: What key role did the stadium's snowplow operated play in the game following the Dolphins win against the Minnesota Vikings?
items.reference: cleared a spot on the snowy field specifically for New England kicker John Smith

items.text: Name five actors that worked with a German cinematographer?
items.reference: George Clooney, Thekla Reuten, Violante Placido, Irina Björklund, and Paolo Bonacelli

items.text: What is the pen name of author Carolyn Janice Cherry, who wrote the Fortress Series?
items.reference: Carolyn Janice Cherry (born September 1, 1942), better known by the pen name C. J. Cherryh,

items.text: Cave-In-Rock, Illinois was a stronghold for serial killer/bandit brothers who operated in which century?
items.reference: who operated in Tennessee, Kentucky, Illinois, and Mississippi, in the late eighteenth century.

items.text: What is the long name of the Government of Ireland Act 1920 that relates to Islam in Northern Ireland?
items.reference: An Act to provide for the better government of Ireland

items.text: Were the board games, Clans and Drunter und Drüber, both created by Leo Colovini?
items.reference: Drunter und Drüber is a multiplayer board game invented by Klaus Teuber

items.text: Casino and Culto A La Vida are cocktails, one of which contains Havana Club 7 and what else?
items.reference: It features Havana Club 7 Añejo Rum and Cranberry juice

items.text: Josey Scott and Ian Watkins were both promising musicians. Which of these talented men was incarcerated, impacting his career with a rock band?
items.reference: Lostprophets disbanded in 2013 after Watkins was charged with sexual offences in late 2012.

items.text: Alan Schechter supervised a woman with her senior thesis entitled what
items.reference: "There Is Only the Fight . . . : An Analysis of the Alinsky Model."

items.reference: "How I Raised, Folded, Bluffed, Flirted, Cursed, and Won Millions at the World Series of Poker"

items.text: Manchester, New Hampshire is a part of the Northeast megalopolis which included what other major cities?
items.reference: Boston, Providence, Hartford, New York City, Philadelphia, Wilmington, Baltimore, and Washington, D.C.

items.text: Red Bend Catholic College contributes 800 students of the 7,560 population of what central west region of south Wales, Australia town?
items.reference: Forbes in the Central West region of New South Wales, Australia

items.reference: Critics' Choice Television Award for Best Supporting Actress in a Comedy Series

items.text: what caused the death of over 250 people in Accra, Ghana?
items.reference: an explosion and a fire occurred at a petrol station

items.text: If the Charhki  Dadri crash was less dangerous than the Tenerife airport disaster, which occured firat?

items.text: South Dade High School is located between what two national parks?
items.reference: Biscayne National Park to the east and Everglades National Park to the west

items.text: The Prussian General Carl von Clausewitz is associated with what type of realism?
items.reference: Modern thinkers associated with classical realism are Carl von Clausewitz

items.text: What city has a football team and had a player from Pago Pago, American Samoa play for it?
items.reference: He is from Pago Pago, American Samoa and played college football at Oregon.

items.text: Which football team does Lewis Young's older brother, Ashley Young, play for?
items.reference: Premier League club Manchester United and the England national team

items.text: For what type of work is the production company for The Year Without a Santa Claus best known?
items.reference: seasonal television specials, particularly its work in stop motion animation

items.text: What are the anchor stores for the shopping mall constructed by C.J. Segerstrom & Sons in California?
items.reference: three Macy's stores, Sears, Nordstrom, Bloomingdale's, and Saks Fifth Avenue

items.reference: to ensure wide visibility and understanding of cases in a region

items.reference: Rumi (30 September 1207 – 17 December 1273), was a 13th-century Persian Sunni Muslim poet

items.text: What distinction is held by the former NBA player who was a member of the Charlotte Hornets during their 1992-93 season and was head coach for the WNBA team Charlotte Sting?
items.reference: shortest player ever to play in the National Basketball Association

items.text: Which band formed in England, Of Montreal or Vib Gyor?
items.reference: Vib Gyor was a band that was formed in Leeds, England.

items.reference: New Zealand, Canada, the United States, the Falkland Islands, and Peru.

items.text: Party of Syrian Unity was established in the wake of a public statement that announced what ?
items.reference: support for the establishment of a "national home for the Jewish people" in Palestine

items.reference: a championship and an NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award

items.text: McLemore Avenue is to Booker T. & the M.G.s as what road in the city of Westminster in London is to the Beatles?
items.reference: This road is best known for the Abbey Road Studios and the 1969 album, "Abbey Road", by The Beatles.

items.text: What German is Arthur Wesley Wheen best known for translating?
items.reference: best known for translating the work of Erich Maria Remarque
